note: Declared input — a reading of ontum's OWN module layering, not a toy. The gate biting reality: does the loop's documented fold-layering survive the change test? Authored from loop/CLAUDE.md's 'Module layering' section, faithfully and NOT tuned to pass. A refusal here is a real finding to surface to bdo, never a thing to edit away.
anchor: reconcile.py — the fold; everything else builds on it

axis.reason: how the append-only log folds into atom state (the derivation rules)
axis.rate: rare — the fold shape is the load-bearing contract

axis.reason: ambient control budgeting — how many steps a tick takes, heat vs cool
axis.rate: occasional — the budgeting policy is tuned as the field teaches
axis.authority: engineer; the setpoint itself is an admitted dial (bdo / the fence)

axis.reason: the setpoint-proposal heuristic — what dial change to propose and the because
axis.rate: occasional — the heuristic evolves as outcomes are read

axis.reason: the disposition policy — admit / escalate / noop a proposal within the fence
axis.rate: occasional — independent of how proposals are generated
axis.authority: engineer owns the policy; bdo owns the fence it executes

pass-and-pressure.crosses: pass_once (the one level-triggered step) and the pressure fold orchestrate senses
pass-and-pressure.trust: trusted: deterministic code in one package, no boundary to cross
pass-and-pressure.change_rate: the fold/pass_once shape changes rarely; the budgeting that consumes it occasionally

tick-history-fold.crosses: the read-only tick/receipt history fold slowloop reads to propose
tick-history-fold.trust: trusted in-process read; slowloop never writes through it
tick-history-fold.change_rate: the fold shape rarely; the proposal heuristic that reads it occasionally

setpoint-proposal.crosses: the proposed setpoint-change record carrying its because/attribution
setpoint-proposal.trust: trusted record; the disposer re-validates it against the admitted fence before acting
setpoint-proposal.authority: engineer owns the proposer; bdo owns the fence the disposer executes (D-4)
setpoint-proposal.change_rate: the proposal record shape rarely; the heuristic and the fence move independently of each other
